en.extract:
The African red-eyed bulbul or black-fronted bulbul (Pycnonotus nigricans) is a species of songbird in the family Pycnonotidae.
It is found in south-western Africa.
Its natural habitats are dry savanna, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, and riverine scrub. It feeds on fruit (including Ficus), flowers, nectar, and insects.


Description
The African red-eyed bulbul ranges from 19 to 21 centimeters (7-8 inches) in length and weighs 30-48 grams (1-1.7 ounces). Its call is a series of loud fluty notes, usually given only by the male.


Taxonomy and systematics
The African red-eyed bulbul was originally described in the genus Turdus. The African red-eyed bulbul is considered to belong to a superspecies along with the Himalayan bulbul, white-eared bulbul, white-spectacled bulbul, Cape bulbul, and the common bulbul. The alternate name of 'red-eyed bulbul' is also used by the Asian red-eyed bulbul.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized:

P. n. nigricans – (Vieillot, 1818): Found in south-western Angola, Namibia and Botswana and western South Africa
P. n. superior – Clancey, 1959: Found in central South Africa


Gallery


References


External links

African red-eyed bulbul – Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.
African red-eyed bulbul Structured guide to the species in southern Africa

Its call is a series of loud fluty notes, usually given only by the male. ==Taxonomy and systematics== The African red-eyed bulbul was originally described in the genus ''[[Turdus]]''. The African red-eyed bulbul is considered to belong to a [[superspecies]] along with the [[Himalayan bulbul]], [[white-eared bulbul]], [[white-spectacled bulbul]], [[Cape bulbul]], and the [[common bulbul]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.hbw.com/species/himalayan-bulbul-pycnonotus-leucogenys|title=Himalayan Bulbul (Pycnonotus leucogenys)|website=hbw.com|language=en|access-date=2017-03-23}} The alternate name of 'red-eyed bulbul' is also used by the [[Asian red-eyed bulbul]]. ===Subspecies=== Two [[subspecies]] are recognized:{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-03-26}} * ''P. n. nigricans'' – ([[Louis Pierre Vieillot|Vieillot]], 1818): Found in south-western Angola, Namibia and Botswana and western South Africa * ''P. n. superior'' – [[Phillip Clancey|Clancey]], 1959: Found in central South Africa ==Gallery== File:127 African red-eyed bulbul in Namib-Naukluft National Park Photo by Giles Laurent.jpg|African red-eyed bulbul in Namib-Naukluft National Park, Namibia File:African red-eyed bulbuls (Pycnonotus nigricans) 2.jpg|''P. n. nigricans''; communal bathing at [[Tswalu Kalahari Reserve]], with one or two keeping watch File:Black-fronted Bulbul, Etosha National Park, Namibia (3926548229).jpg|Nominate race at [[Etosha National Park|Etosha N.
